entries.title: Separate merge, release, deployment, and production verification evidence
entries.summary: Delivery state was previously at risk of being inferred from an earlier stage instead of verified at every boundary.
entries.proposed_change: Require separate fresh evidence for PR merge, GitHub Release, deployment workflow, OCI SHA, health, and critical smoke.

validation.summary: The v0.6.2 deployment independently verified workflow success, OCI SHA, health, authenticated smoke, and GitNexus refresh.

entries.title: Degrade gracefully when GitNexus is noisy or low value
entries.summary: Repeated GitNexus queries can consume time and tokens when FTS, worktree identity, or untracked-file mapping is degraded.
entries.proposed_change: Use GitNexus only when it reduces uncertainty, refresh once when needed, and record a fallback using Git diff, source reading, and tests.

validation.summary: The wrapper preserves Git changed-file evidence and explicitly marks low-value graph results.

entries.title: Use verification gates matched to the development boundary
entries.summary: Running broad regression and E2E after every edit made verification cost exceed implementation value.
entries.proposed_change: Use focused checks during development, broad CI at PR boundaries, and production verification only at release boundaries.

validation.summary: The standard defines distinct development, PR, integration, release, and production evidence.

entries.title: Validate user-visible discoverability, not only implementation existence
entries.summary: A capability can exist in code and still be effectively unavailable when the default route, tab, or interaction hides it.
entries.proposed_change: Add user-discoverability assertions to acceptance guidance for navigation, default views, visible feedback, and callable actions.

validation.summary: The tenant member list existed but was hidden behind a non-default tab. A normal-entry-path Playwright assertion failed before the fix, passed after the default-view change, and the capability passed CI plus authenticated production smoke in deployment run 27500901328.

entries.title: Do not confuse PowerShell console mojibake with source encoding corruption
entries.summary: Console rendering previously suggested corrupted Chinese source even though strict UTF-8 and browser output were correct.
entries.proposed_change: Require strict UTF-8 source inspection and rendered browser evidence before changing encoding or user-visible copy.

validation.summary: The verification rule now explicitly separates console rendering from source and browser evidence.

entries.title: Align merge, release, deployment, and Batch governance authority
entries.summary: Repository rules, runbooks, release evidence, and product docs used inconsistent language for agent merge authority, Owner Go, release promotion, deployment gates, and Batch completion.
entries.proposed_change: Make Low-risk documentation-only PR auto-merge explicit, require Owner Go for product/API/security/migration/Docker/workflow/release/deployment/production PRs, preserve full release/deploy gates, and require standardized Batch completion checklists.

entries.title: Prefer bounded forward diagnosis over repeated rollback loops
entries.summary: Repeated production deployment failures were initially handled with repeated rollback attempts even after evidence showed the same readiness blocker persisted. The effective path was bounded diagnosis, schema compatibility fixes, and aligning production deployment gates with candidate gates.
entries.proposed_change: When a production rollback or redeploy repeats the same gate failure, stop additional rollback loops, preserve evidence, compare candidate and production gates, and create the smallest forward compatibility or workflow fix before retrying deployment.

validation.summary: Forward-fix governance now requires stopping repeated rollback loops for the same production gate failure, preserving evidence, comparing candidate and production gates, classifying the failure, and retrying only after CI and Owner Go while preserving normal rollback for unrelated catastrophic failures.
